D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Weight Buys Contrast

thin type needs strong contrast; heavy type can read at a fraction of it — the stroke width does some of the work

Contrast is not the whole story of legibility — stroke width matters too. A hairline weight needs high APCA Lc to survive; a bold weight stays readable at far lower contrast because more ink reaches the eye. APCA encodes this: the same Lc clears a smaller minimum size as weight rises.

Set the text-vs-background contrast. Each weight is shown live with a pass/fail badge at a fixed body size — watch the thin weights fail first as you lower the contrast, while the heavy ones hold on.
